title: strychnos umbellata
intro: Strychnos umbellata, also known as the white strychnine tree, is a species of flowering plant in the Loganiaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The plant's medicinal properties are derived from its seeds, which contain a toxic alkaloid that has been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments. The health benefits of Strychnos umbellata include its antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat fever, skin infections, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often dried and powdered for use in traditional remedies, although they should be used with caution due to their toxicity.
